birth_date.description: birth_date in YYYY/M/D format with partial dates allowed (e.g., 1839/1/9, 1993/4, or 1900), or leave empty if not applicable

death_date.description: death_date in YYYY/M/D format with partial dates allowed (e.g., 1839/1/9, 1993/4, or 1900), or leave empty if not applicable

age.description: age of the artist (if death_date is given, compute as death_date - birth_date; otherwise, compute as 2025/1/1 - birth_date)

century.description: century when the artist was active or influential (e.g., 19th or 19th–20th; can span multiple centuries)

zodiac.description: western zodiac sign based on birth date, choose only one from [Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Sagittarius, Capricorn, Aquarius, Pisces]

birth_country.description: country where the artist was born (e.g., netherlands)

birth_city.description: city where the artist was born

birth_continent.description: continent where the artist was born, select one from [Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Oceania]

death_country.description: country where the artist died, or leave empty if not applicable (e.g., switzerland)

death_city.description: city where the artist died, or leave empty if not applicable

field.description: primary artistic field, choose one or more from: [Painting, Sculpture, Printmaking, Drawing, Photography, Illustration, Collage, Textile art, Installation, Performance, Ceramics, Digital art, Architecture, Mosaic, Graphic Design, Calligraphy], seperated by `||` (e.g. Collage || Illustration || Textile art).

genre.description: primary genre of work, choose one or more from: [Portrait, Landscape, Still Life, Religious, Mythological, History, genre, Nude, Animal, Illustration, Allegorical, Abstract, Fantasy, Sketch, Figurative, Conceptual, Surrealist, Symbolic, Political, Social Realism, Pop art, Narrative, Environmental, Sculpture, Architecture, Music, Film, Digital art, Literature, Other], seperated by `||` (e.g. Portrait || Political).

marriage.description: marital status of the artist, reflecting the artist’s latest (final) marital status as mentioned in the biography. Choose one from: [Cohabiting, Divorced, Married, Remarried, Separated, Unmarried, Widowed, Unknown]. Only extract and fill in the last marital status referenced in the text.

art_institution.description: art academies, schools, organizations, associations, or exhibition societies the artist was affiliated with, taught at, or participated in (e.g., national art academies, artist unions, art schools, exhibition societies).

teaching.description: whether the artist taught at an institution (1 if yes, 0 if none)

awards.description: Number of significant and clearly documented awards the artist received (enter an integer; use 0 if none; exclude medals, scholarships, or honorary mentions).

style.description: the overall artistic style of the image. Choose one or more from: [Abstraction, Cubism, Realism, Impressionism, Modernism, Design, Conceptual, Environmental, Popular, Photography, Digital, Outsider, Asian, Misc, Other]; if multiple styles are clearly present, separate them with `||`.

image_genre.description: the artistic genre or type of the image, choose one or more from: [Abstraction, Geometry, Conceptual, Figuration, Portraiture, Landscape, Urban, Street, Interior, Architecture, Narrative, Imagination, Religion, History, Politics, Animals, Action, StillLife, Medium, Graphic, Industry, Text, Other]; if multiple genres are relevant, separate them with `||`.

object.description: the primary visible objects or subjects depicted in the image (e.g., woman, tree, city skyline, animals, abstract shapes). List the key visual elements as nouns or short noun phrases; if there are several, separate them with `||`.

color.description: the dominant or characteristic colors of the image; Choose one or more from: [Grey, Gray, Black, White, Yellow, Red, Pink, Brown, Orange, Green, Blue, Purple, Gold, Silver, Other]; if multiple colors are important, separate them with `||`.

tone.description: the overall tonal and emotional quality of the image, Choose one or more from: [Bold,Bright,Calm,Contrast,Dark,Dramatic,Dreamlike,Dynamic,Ethereal,Light,Minimalist,Minimalistic,Muted,Natural,Neutral,Soft,Somber,Subdued,Vibrant,Warm,Other]; if multiple tones are relevant, separate them with `||`.

composition.description: the way visual elements are arranged in the image (e.g., asymmetrical with central focus, symmetrical and balanced, dynamic diagonal composition, crowded and layered, minimal with large negative space). Describe the composition style in one short free-form phrase.
